assertions.text: V1 was entering the roadway from a parking spot.
Roadway has five lanes and is two-way.
V1 attempted to make a U-turn to head west.
V2 was traveling east in lane two of the five-lane roadway.
As V1 made the U-turn, the left side of V1 made contact with V2's front plane.
As the vehicles continued in motion after the initial contact, the left side of V1 struck the front of V2.
Initial contact was between the left side of V1 and the front plane of V2.
Subsequent contact was between the left side of V1 and the front of V2.
There were at least two distinct contacts: initial contact of left side V1 to V2 front plane, then left side V1 striking V2 front as they continued moving.
V1 came to final rest in westbound lanes of travel.
V2 came to final rest in the middle of the roadway.
V1 was moving (making a U-turn) when contacts occurred.

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry is from V2 and contains no sensors or data about V1's position or motion; therefore whether "V1 was entering the roadway from a parking spot" is unverifiable from these sensors. No quantitative claim to cite.
Roadway geometry ("five lanes and is two-way") is not available in V2 telemetry; unverifiable from sensors alone.
V2 telemetry contains no direct information about V1's maneuver (U-turn heading west); unverifiable from the provided channels.
V2 speed and heading sensors do not report lane-level position; lane two and direction east for V2 cannot be determined from the telemetry alone; unverifiable.

Impact geometry describing which side of V1 contacted which part of V2 is not directly reported by V2 telemetry. The crash-pulse shows a longitudinal cumulative delta-V of [ -18.0 km/h ](T2) and lateral delta-V of [ -5.0 km/h ](T2), but axis alignment to vehicle sides is undocumented so side-of-other-vehicle contact is unverifiable.

Telemetry shows a crash pulse with final longitudinal delta-V [ -18.0 km/h ](T2) and lateral delta-V final [ -2.0 km/h ](T2) and an impact delta-v peak times at [0.1 s](T1) and [0.07 s](T1), but whether a separate subsequent contact (left side of V1 striking V2 front) occurred is not distinguishable; therefore unverifiable.

The device-frame delta-V peaks are [ -18.0 km/h longitudinal at 0.1 s ](T2) and [ -5.0 km/h lateral at 0.07 s ](T2), but device axes to vehicle left/right/front are undocumented so initial contact described as left-side V1 to front-plane V2 is unverifiable from V2 sensors alone.
Subsequent contact direction is not separable in the measured delta-V curve; telemetry does not record which part of the other vehicle struck V2, so unverifiable.

Telemetric detections show a single crash event with delta-V peaks in longitudinal [ -18.0 km/h ](T2) and lateral [ -5.0 km/h ](T2) channels and the EDR records one event ("num_events_recorded": 1) in the summary (T1), which contradicts multiple distinct crash events but does not prove multiple contacts between vehicles; thus the claim of at least two distinct contacts is unverifiable.
Final rest position (V1 in westbound lanes) is not reported in V2 telemetry; unverifiable.
V2 final rest location in the roadway is not present in the telemetry; unverifiable.

V2 pre-crash speed increases from [5.0 km/h at t=-5.0 s] to [35.0 km/h at t=0.0 s] (T2), steering shows a peak deviation of [30.0 deg at t=-0.5 s] (T2), and a brake transition occurred at [t=-0.5 s] (T2), indicating V2 was in motion when the impact occurred. The crash pulse shows a longitudinal delta-V peaking at [ -18.0 km/h at t=0.1 s ](T2), consistent with an in-motion collision (T1).

result.rationale: Most assertions describe V1's maneuvers, lane geometry, and relative contact locations which are not recorded by V2 telemetry; the sensors provide vehicle V2 motion and a single crash pulse (longitudinal [ -18.0 km/h ](T2), lateral [ -5.0 km/h ](T2)) but do not document other-vehicle positions or multi-contact sequences. Only that V2 was moving at impact is supported.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

accel_pedal.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
